issues.title: Integer overflow in msg_data_bytes calculation from KAT file
issues.problem: When msg_len_bits_val from a KAT file is close to ULLONG_MAX, the expression (msg_len_bits_val + 7) / 8 can overflow, resulting in a small value that passes allocation checks but causes buffer overflow later.

issues.title: No upper bound check for msg_bytes before malloc
issues.problem: msg_bytes is computed from KAT file data and allocated directly without checking against NGCC_MAX_BUFFER_LEN. A malicious KAT file could cause allocation of arbitrary size, potentially exhausting memory or causing denial of service.

issues.title: SM3_DRNG_Instantiate doesn't validate nonce_len_bytes before using in malloc/memcpy
issues.problem: nonce_len_bytes is used directly in malloc without validation. If an attacker-controlled large value is passed, it could cause integer overflow in the malloc size or excessive memory allocation. Additionally, memcpy at line 251 writes into a buffer that might be smaller than nonce_len_bytes if SEEDLEN > nonce_len_bytes.

issues.title: KAT parser now rejects previously supported field names and single-file inputs
issues.problem: The new implementation only recognizes specific field names (Msg/Dst, PK/M/Sn, SK/CT/SS, SKa/PKb/PKa/SKb/SS, Init_Sta/Init_Stb) inside files with specific prefixes (KAT_HASH_, KAT_SIG_, KAT_KEM_, KAT_KEX_) under a directory. Previously supported aliases (INPUT/OUTPUT/MSG/DIGEST/PUBLICKEY/SECRETKEY/etc.) and single-file --kat inputs are now rejected or skipped.

issues.title: Public performance CLI flags removed without compatibility path
issues.problem: This removes --iterations, --msg-len, and --cycles from the parser, so existing scripts and tests now fail before any benchmark runs. The branch still has callers that invoke those flags, and users lose the ability to control benchmark size/count from CLI.
issues.code:
static const struct option long_options[] = {
    {"lib", required_argument, NULL, 'l'},
    {"test", required_argument, NULL, 't'},
    {"mode", required_argument, NULL, 'm'},
    {"digest-len-bits", required_argument, NULL, 'b'},
    {"duration-hours", required_argument, NULL, 'd'},
    {"stability-max-cases", required_argument, NULL, 's'},
    {"stability-sample-ms", required_argument, NULL, OPT_STABILITY_SAMPLE_MS},
    {"json-out", required_argument, NULL, 'j'},
    {"kat", required_argument, NULL, 'k'},
    ...
};

issues.title: JSON writer no longer emits the required v4 option fields
issues.problem: The report now omits options.iterations, options.msg_len, options.digest_len_bits, and options.cycles, so the bundled validator immediately fails with missing key 'iterations'. The generated report is incompatible with the schema shipped in this repo.

issues.title: Total test count not incremented on validation failure
issues.problem: When a test vector fails the _Len field validation, (*io_failed) is incremented but (*io_total) is never incremented. This results in inaccurate test statistics where failed can exceed total.

issues.title: Total test count not incremented on validation failure
issues.problem: When a test vector fails the _Len field validation, (*io_failed) is incremented but (*io_total) is never incremented. This results in inaccurate test statistics where failed can exceed total.

issues.title: Total test count not incremented on validation failures
issues.problem: In verify_kex_kat_vectors, when length validation fails, (*io_failed) is incremented but (*io_total) is not, leading to incorrect statistics where failed vectors are not counted as part of the total cases.

issues.title: DSA sign and verify sub-operations only benchmark first message size
issues.problem: The loop break condition treats all sub-operations as message-length-independent, but ngcc_dsa_sig_performance() and ngcc_dsa_verify_performance() both take msg_len and scale bytes_per_op with it. DSA_KEYGEN, DSA_SIG, and DSA_VERIFY only run the 1024-byte case.

issues.title: KAT_LOOP_ITERATIONS runs 1,000,000 times without progress indication
issues.problem: The KAT loop test runs 1,000,000 iterations without any progress output. For a slow hash implementation or large message size, this could take hours and appear to be hung. No timeout mechanism exists.

issues.title: get_random_number doesn't validate input pointers for NULL
issues.problem: The function doesn't check if drng or random_number are NULL before dereferencing them. This could cause null pointer dereference crashes.

issues.title: Missing newline at end of file
issues.problem: The file ends without a newline character, which violates POSIX standards and can cause issues with some tools and version control systems.

issues.title: Missing newline at end of file
issues.problem: The header file ends without a newline character, which violates POSIX standards and can cause issues with some tools and version control systems.
